name: Pork with Mustard Sauce
recipeInstructions: Cut the pork tenderloin into eight pieces. Slice each piece again, but do not cut all of the way through; flatten each piece by pounding slightly with a meat mallet. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook the pork in butter until no longer pink, 5 to 6 minutes per side. Remove the pork to a serving dish and keep warm; discard the drippings. In the same skillet, cook the broth and tarragon over high heat until reduced by half. Reduce the heat, stir in the cream and mustard. Season with salt and pepper. Spoon the sauce over the pork. Serve with noodles if desired.
